deconstruction_targets: unordained laymen and secular clerics attempting exorcism
private use of charms and benedictions without proper conditions
the legitimacy and methodology of exorcism itself
constructive_proposals: judging the legality of exorcism by ordained status
observing seven conditions for private charm and benediction use
proper method of exorcising disease and conjuring the devil
actions to take when no healing grace results from exorcism
practical, non-verbal remedies and solutions to theological arguments
summary: The text deconstructs the legitimacy of exorcism by questioning who may perform it, outlining strict conditions for private rituals, and examining procedural failures, all within a highly charged moral framework that assumes witchcraft causes real physical illness. While it offers structured proposals for correct practice, it provides no constructive theological or administrative blueprint beyond reinforcing clerical authority and ritual correctness.
